label: The Driver & The Driver
communicationTipA: Yield on small decisions so both of you can move fast — competing on every detail slows everything down.
communicationTipB: Pick your battles — not every decision needs to become a negotiation.
bestFormat: Quick standups with clear action items — avoid circular debates.

label: The Driver & The Influencer
communicationTipA: Give them room to socialize — their relationships open doors. Focus on outcomes, let them handle the buy-in.
communicationTipB: Bring data and conclusions first, then share the story. They want results, not the journey.
bestFormat: Brief framework meetings with freedom to iterate between syncs.

label: The Driver & The Supporter
communicationTipA: Slow down, give context, ask for their input — they have valuable perspectives they may not volunteer.
communicationTipB: Lead with conclusions, then provide details. They appreciate knowing the destination before the route.
bestFormat: Structured 1:1s with a shared agenda and time for questions.

label: The Driver & The Analyst
communicationTipA: Give them time to analyze — their precision catches what speed misses. Set clear deadlines to balance thoroughness with momentum.
communicationTipB: Provide your analysis upfront so they can decide quickly. They value accuracy over speed.
bestFormat: Async briefs with written decisions — let the Analyst process before responding.

label: The Influencer & The Driver
communicationTipA: Respect their drive for results — support their goals with your people skills and they will value your contribution.
communicationTipB: Let them warm up the room — their charm opens doors that directness cannot.
bestFormat: Goal-oriented check-ins with space for relationship-building.

label: The Influencer & The Influencer
communicationTipA: Create accountability structures — both of you generate ideas, but someone needs to follow through.
communicationTipB: Divide enthusiasm from execution — take turns driving follow-through so neither person bears the whole burden.
bestFormat: Creative brainstorming sessions with a designated note-taker and action items.

label: The Influencer & The Supporter
communicationTipA: Give them space to share — they have thoughtful ideas they may not voice in an enthusiastic crowd.
communicationTipB: Appreciate their warmth and consistency — they create the stable environment your ideas need to land.
bestFormat: Collaborative discussions with action items documented clearly.

label: The Influencer & The Analyst
communicationTipA: Bring data, not just stories — they need evidence to buy in, and they will respect you for respecting their process.
communicationTipB: Lead with a brief personal connection, then get to the point — they appreciate warmth but value efficiency.
bestFormat: Written proposals with optional verbal walkthrough — gives the Analyst time to process.

label: The Supporter & The Driver
communicationTipA: State your needs clearly — they will not notice what you do not say, and your silence may be read as agreement.
communicationTipB: Give them time to process — they think things through before responding, which is not resistance.
bestFormat: Brief check-ins with a written agenda shared in advance.

label: The Supporter & The Influencer
communicationTipA: Appreciate their energy and let them take the social lead — they create momentum you can support with consistency.
communicationTipB: Value their reliability — they are the steady force that turns your ideas into sustainable practices.
bestFormat: Regular team syncs with clear next steps.

label: The Supporter & The Supporter
communicationTipA: Push each other to make decisions — your mutual desire for consensus can slow progress.
communicationTipB: Name what needs to change, even when it is uncomfortable — maintaining harmony at the cost of progress helps no one.
bestFormat: Structured meetings with rotation of who drives the agenda.

label: The Supporter & The Analyst
communicationTipA: Ask what they need to feel confident in a decision — their analysis is a gift, not a delay.
communicationTipB: Share your reasoning — they want to understand the process, not just the conclusion.
bestFormat: Written summaries with agreed timelines for decisions.

label: The Analyst & The Driver
communicationTipA: Present your analysis as a tool for faster decisions — they value precision that leads to action.
communicationTipB: Give them a deadline for analysis so they do not hold up your momentum seeking perfection.
bestFormat: Async written briefs with a decision meeting at the end.

label: The Analyst & The Influencer
communicationTipA: Let them build rapport first — people listen to data they receive from someone they trust.
communicationTipB: Respect their need for precision — their thoroughness makes your ideas actually work.
bestFormat: Written analyses discussed in person — the Analyst preps, the Influencer presents.

label: The Analyst & The Supporter
communicationTipA: Make your reasoning accessible — they want to understand the why behind the what, not just the data.
communicationTipB: Give them time and space to present their analysis — they have done the work and want it heard.
bestFormat: Structured meetings with written pre-reads and time for discussion.

label: The Analyst & The Analyst
communicationTipA: Agree on a decision framework early — otherwise both of you will keep creating more analysis.
communicationTipB: Set a hard stop for research and start executing — two perfectionists can spiral into analysis paralysis.
bestFormat: Async document reviews with a forced decision deadline.
